name: Plausible Analytics
tagline: Privacy-friendly website analytics without cookies or consent banners
description: Plausible is a lightweight, open-source web analytics tool that gives you meaningful traffic stats without collecting personal data or using cookies. Fully GDPR-compliant and under 1KB, it shows everything you need on a single clean dashboard. Built as a direct, ethical alternative to Google Analytics.

name: Fathom Analytics
tagline: Stop feeding Google your visitors' data — get simple, private analytics
description: Fathom is a privacy-first Google Analytics alternative that gives you traffic insights without cookies, tracking scripts, or GDPR headaches. It routes through your own subdomain so ad blockers rarely touch it. Self-funded from day one by a two-person team.

name: Buttondown
tagline: The best way to start, grow, and run your newsletter
description: Buttondown is a small, elegant newsletter tool for writers and indie makers who want a focused, no-bloat experience. It strips away the complexity of Mailchimp and lets you focus on writing great emails and growing your audience. Grown entirely through word-of-mouth since 2017.

name: EmailOctopus
tagline: Email marketing that does more for less
description: EmailOctopus is an affordable email marketing platform offering campaigns, automations, and landing pages at a fraction of Mailchimp's price. Built because existing tools were too expensive for small businesses. Bootstrapped to over $3M ARR with no investors.

name: Invoice Ninja
tagline: Free invoicing, expenses, and time-tracking for freelancers
description: Invoice Ninja is a fully-featured, source-available invoicing platform that lets freelancers and small businesses create invoices, track expenses, manage time, and accept payments. Offers both hosted cloud and self-hostable versions. Used by over 200,000 businesses worldwide.

name: Cushion
tagline: See your freelance income and workload before it happens
description: Cushion combines scheduling, invoicing, time-tracking, and income forecasting in one app built specifically for freelancers. It helps you visualise upcoming income and avoid the feast-or-famine cycle. Solo-built by a design engineer in Brooklyn who's been building it in public since 2014.

name: Crisp
tagline: All-in-one customer messaging — live chat, email, WhatsApp, and more
description: Crisp brings together live chat, email, WhatsApp, Instagram, Telegram, AI agents, a knowledge base, and CRM into a single workspace. Founded in Nantes, France with zero VC funding ever raised, it now serves over 200,000 brands worldwide. Free plan available.

name: HelpKit
tagline: Turn your Notion pages into a professional help centre
description: HelpKit lets you build a polished, public-facing knowledge base directly from your existing Notion pages — no coding required. Perfect for indie makers and small SaaS teams who already use Notion. Solo-bootstrapped to over $10K MRR by a self-taught developer from Austria.

name: SavvyCal
tagline: Scheduling links that let people overlay their own calendar
description: SavvyCal replaces the clunky Calendly experience with a calendar overlay — recipients see your availability on top of their own schedule and pick the best mutual time. Built by the former technical co-founder of Drip. Small-team, bootstrapped, and backed by TinySeed.

name: Canny
tagline: Capture and prioritise feature requests without the spreadsheet chaos
description: Canny lets your users submit and upvote feature requests, helps you prioritise what to build, and automatically notifies everyone when you ship. Founded by two ex-Facebook engineers who bootstrapped it to $3.3M ARR with zero outside investment over seven years.

name: Senja
tagline: Collect and display testimonials that actually convert visitors
description: Senja makes it easy to request testimonials via a branded form, then display them anywhere — embedded widgets, wall-of-love pages, or auto-formatted social posts. Built remotely by two indie hackers who met online and bootstrapped it to $1M ARR with over 3,000 customers.

name: Tally
tagline: Create beautiful forms as easily as typing a document — free forever
description: Tally is a Notion-style form builder where you type your form like a document instead of dragging blocks around. The generous free tier made it a go-to for indie makers and small businesses who find Typeform too expensive and Google Forms too ugly. Bootstrapped to $4M ARR by a two-person team in Belgium.

name: Typefully
tagline: Write, schedule, and grow on X, LinkedIn, Bluesky, and Threads
description: Typefully started as a tiny side project for writing Twitter threads, went viral on Hacker News, and grew into a $1.6M ARR scheduling and analytics tool. Now covers X, LinkedIn, Threads, Bluesky, and Mastodon with AI writing assistance and analytics built in. Fully bootstrapped by two Italian founders.

name: Carrd
tagline: Build a beautiful one-page site in minutes — from $19/year
description: Carrd is the beloved one-page website builder made by one person. Instead of competing with Wix or Squarespace, it went ruthlessly narrow: one page, done beautifully. Grown entirely by word of mouth to 4M+ sites and $1.5M+ ARR with no marketing team, ads, or investors.

name: Oh Dear
tagline: Monitor your whole website, not just whether it's up
description: Oh Dear goes beyond basic uptime pings — it monitors SSL certificate expiry, broken links, DNS changes, scheduled tasks, and application health from one dashboard. Built by two developers from Antwerp as a hobby project in 2017, it's been self-funded and profitable ever since.
maker_name: Freek Van der Herten & Mattias Geniar

name: ScreenshotOne
tagline: A screenshot API that handles ad blockers, cookies, and full-page scrolls
description: ScreenshotOne turns any URL or raw HTML into a screenshot via a simple API — handling ad blocking, cookie banners, and full-page captures so you don't have to manage Puppeteer yourself. Solo-built to $10K MRR and over 1 million screenshots generated.

name: Pika
tagline: Turn boring screenshots into polished, shareable mockups instantly
description: Pika wraps your screenshots in browser frames, device mockups, or gradient backgrounds to make them share-worthy for Product Hunt launches, social posts, and docs. Solo-built by a design engineer from India, with no employees or marketing spend. Runs on a freemium model.

name: Activepieces
tagline: Open-source automation with 330+ integrations — self-host or use the cloud
description: Activepieces is an open-source automation platform rivalling Zapier with a visual workflow builder and native AI agent support. MIT-licensed so you can self-host for free on Docker and never pay per task. About 60% of its integrations were contributed by the community.
